title: Industrial dependency graph OS for factories and critical suppliers
reason: Every database so far points back to supplier visibility, digital thread, OT assets and bottlenecks. A graph OS that maps machines, suppliers, software, OT assets, data flows, compliance and production dependencies can serve aerospace, defence, energy and manufacturing.

title: IEC 62443 continuous evidence and patch-status platform
reason: ISA/IEC 62443 creates continuous evidence needs around IACS security. A lightweight platform can track assets, zones/conduits, patch state, vendor SBOMs, controls and audit evidence across OT environments.

title: Unified Namespace builder and data-quality monitor
reason: Factories need contextual OT data before AI or analytics can work. A UNS builder that validates MQTT/Sparkplug/OPC UA streams, naming, units, lineage and missing metadata attacks a real bottleneck.

title: Brownfield machine connector marketplace
reason: Brownfield factories are full of legacy PLCs, machines and proprietary protocols. A verified connector marketplace with templates, drivers, test harnesses and integrator reviews can reduce IT/OT integration cost.

title: AI-ready factory score and remediation engine
reason: Industrial AI fails without data quality, asset models, connectivity, security and operator trust. A score/remediation engine can tell plants exactly what blocks AI deployment and what to fix first.

title: Simulation-to-MES bridge for validated production change
reason: Simulation is powerful but often disconnected from the shop floor. A bridge from simulation/digital-twin outputs into MES, recipes, work instructions and change-control can create high-value industrial workflow software.

title: PLC and machine-parameter change-control vault
reason: PLC code, recipes and machine parameters often change without strong unified audit, rollback and impact analysis. A vendor-neutral change-control vault is accessible and painful.

title: Frontline-to-digital-thread work instruction platform
reason: Operators and technicians generate real evidence, deviations, fixes and tribal knowledge. Connecting frontline work instructions to PLM/MES/quality records can close digital-thread gaps without replacing big systems.

title: OT asset intelligence for procurement and cyber insurance
reason: OT asset data is valuable beyond security: procurement, lifecycle planning, insurance, maintenance and modernization. A normalized asset intelligence layer can sit on top of security discovery tools.

title: Machine-level energy and emissions digital twin
reason: Factories need energy visibility at asset/line/product level. A machine-level energy/emissions twin can connect meters, machines, work orders and product carbon reporting.

title: Industrial vendor-lock-in translator and migration assistant
reason: Factories struggle to migrate between vendor stacks because tags, recipes, screens, PLC code, reports and histories are trapped. A translator/migration assistant can reduce switching friction.

title: Industrial software opportunity engine for founders and investors
reason: This cross-cutting layer exposes where AI, defence, aerospace and manufacturing all hit the same bottlenecks: data context, traceability, compliance, OT security, simulation-to-shopfloor and supplier visibility.
